questions.question: Scenario: Your company operates in three domains – airlines, hotels, and ride-hailing services. Each domain has two teams – analytics and data science, which generate data assets in BigQuery with the support of a central data platform team. However, the rapid evolution of each domain is leading to the central data platform team becoming a bottleneck, causing delays in deriving insights and resulting in stale data when pipelines are not maintained. How can you utilize Dataplex to implement a data mesh architecture and eliminate the bottleneck in your organization‘s data operations?
options: 1. Create one lake for each team. Inside each lake, create one zone for each domain. 2. Attach each of the BigQuery datasets created by the individual teams as assets to the respective zone. 3. Have the central data platform team manage all zones data assets.

1. Create one lake for each team. Inside each lake, create one zone for each domain. 2. Attach each of the BigQuery datasets created by the individual teams as assets to the respective zone. 3. Direct each domain to manage their own zones data assets.

1. Create one lake for each domain. Inside each lake, create one zone for each team. 2. Attach each of the BigQuery datasets created by the individual teams as assets to the respective zone. 3. Direct each domain to manage their own lakes data assets.

1. Create one lake for each domain. Inside each lake, create one zone for each team. 2. Attach each of the BigQuery datasets created by the individual teams as assets to the respective zone. 3. Have the central data platform team manage all lakes data assets.
answer: 1. Create one lake for each domain. Inside each lake, create one zone for each team. 2. Attach each of the BigQuery datasets created by the individual teams as assets to the respective zone. 3. Direct each domain to manage their own lakes data assets.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option C.Option C suggests creating one lake for each domain, which aligns with the data mesh architecture principles of decentralization and domain-driven data ownership. Inside each lake, a zone should be created for each team, allowing for team-specific data management. By attaching BigQuery datasets created by individual teams as assets to the respective zone, data can be effectively managed and accessed within each domain. Directing each domain to manage their own lake‘s data assets enables autonomy and agility in data management, reducing dependencies on the central data platform team and eliminating bottlenecks.Why the other options are incorrect:A. This option suggests having the central data platform team manage all zones‘ data assets, which goes against the principles of decentralization in a data mesh architecture. It does not address the bottleneck issue caused by the central team‘s involvement in managing all data assets.B. Similar to option A, this option directs each domain to manage their own zone‘s data assets, but still retains the central data platform team as the manager of all zones. This does not effectively address the bottleneck issue and does not promote domain-driven data ownership.D. This option proposes having the central data platform team manage all lakes‘ data assets, which centralizes data management and does not align with the principles of a data mesh architecture. It does not solve the bottleneck problem and does not promote domain-driven data ownership and autonomy.
